patterns.title: Creating a Fruit Store Landing Page with React and CSS
patterns.summary: This trajectory outlines the steps to build a basic landing page for a fruit store using React and CSS, including structuring the HTML, displaying product data, and applying styling.
patterns.distillation: When building a simple landing page, start by structuring the HTML with header, main (containing hero and product list sections), and footer. Use CSS to style the page, focusing on layout (e.g., grid for product listing), and visual appeal (e.g., button styling). Use React components and data mapping to render dynamic content efficiently.

patterns.title: React Landing Page Structure for E-commerce
patterns.summary: This trajectory outlines a pattern for structuring a React landing page for an e-commerce store, including component breakdown, data handling, and styling.
patterns.distillation: When creating e-commerce landing pages in React, follow these steps: 1. Define product data structure in `src/data.ts` with fields like `id`, `name`, `price`, and `imageUrl`. 2. Create reusable components in `src/components` such as `Header`, `Hero`, `ProductCard`, `ProductList`, and `Footer`. 3. Implement responsive grid layouts using CSS for `ProductList`. 4. Centralize global styling in `src/index.css` and component-specific styling in dedicated CSS files or modules. 5. Import and render all components within `App.tsx` to assemble the landing page.

patterns.title: React E-commerce Landing Page Structure
patterns.summary: This trajectory outlines a pattern for building an e-commerce landing page using React, including component structure, data management, and styling.
patterns.distillation: When building e-commerce landing pages with React, follow a component-based approach by creating components for the header, hero section, product cards, product list, and footer; manage product data in a separate data file; and use CSS Grid for responsive product listing.

patterns.title: Web App Development for Building Supply Store
patterns.summary: The agent successfully developed a web application for a building supply store by iteratively writing and verifying code using internal coder agents and terminal bot agents, culminating in a browser preview.
patterns.distillation: For building similar web applications, utilize a process of iterative coding and verification, leveraging internal coder agents for code generation and terminal bots for execution of commands; ensure a browser preview is captured to confirm successful deployment and functionality.

patterns.title: Successful landing page creation for a mountain bike shop.
patterns.summary: The agent successfully created a landing page for a mountain bike shop by creating a new folder, writing and verifying code multiple times, and capturing a browser preview.
patterns.distillation: When creating landing pages, use the following process: create a new directory for the project, iterate on code development and verification, and finally, preview the page in a browser to ensure it meets requirements. Leverage internal coder agents and terminal bots to streamline development.

patterns.title: Successful Landing Page Generation
patterns.summary: The agent successfully generated a landing page for an athletic shoe store on the first attempt.
patterns.distillation: This indicates a robust and reliable coding process for creating landing pages, especially in the e-commerce domain, suggesting a potentially reusable code template or a highly effective prompt engineering strategy.

patterns.title: Successful website creation for a sports shoe store
patterns.summary: The agent successfully created a professional website for a sports shoe store through a sequence of terminal commands, code writing, and browser preview.
patterns.distillation: When building websites, prioritize using a combination of terminal commands for setup, internal code writing and verification, and browser previews for quality assurance to ensure successful project completion.

patterns.title: Terminal Bot Inaction on Completion
patterns.summary: The terminal bot skipped its turn because the project sequence was already completed.
patterns.distillation: Ensure terminal bot has a defined action, even after project completion, such as verification or final report generation to prevent skipped turns.

patterns.title: Successful Project Sequence Completion
patterns.summary: The project sequence was completed successfully.
patterns.distillation: A successful trajectory indicates the project sequence was executed without issues; maintaining consistent execution protocols will likely lead to continued success.

patterns.title: Successful Project Completion
patterns.summary: The project sequence to create a modern shoe store website was successfully completed.
patterns.distillation: When a project sequence completes successfully, document the steps taken by terminal_bot for future reference.

resolved_errors.title: Coder Trae Timeout Issue
resolved_errors.summary: The coder agent, Trae, timed out while attempting to implement the landing page code, indicating a potential problem with task complexity or resource allocation.
resolved_errors.distillation: When assigning UI development tasks, ensure the timeframe is adequate for the complexity, and that the coding agent has sufficient resources or processing power to complete the task within the allotted time. Consider breaking down complex UI tasks into smaller, more manageable sub-tasks.

resolved_errors.title: Coder Internal Repeated Failures
resolved_errors.summary: The coder_internal agent repeatedly failed to generate the landing page code, even after multiple self-healing attempts, leading to project failure.
resolved_errors.distillation: When the coder_internal agent fails repeatedly (e.g., more than 3 times) on a coding task despite self-healing, escalate to a different strategy, such as using a different coding approach or assigning the task to a human coder, rather than continuing with the same failing method.

resolved_errors.title: Coder Internal consistently fails after initial success.
resolved_errors.summary: The coder_internal agent initially succeeds in writing code but then fails repeatedly with self-healing attempts, indicating a potential issue with code stability or environmental dependencies.
resolved_errors.distillation: Investigate the root cause of coder_internal's failure after the initial code generation. Consider implementing more robust error handling, better dependency management, or more thorough testing procedures before deploying code. Monitor the self-healing attempts to identify recurring error patterns and address them proactively.

resolved_errors.title: Coder_internal fails repeatedly with self-healing
resolved_errors.summary: The coder_internal agent failed repeatedly after multiple self-healing attempts, leading to project failure.
resolved_errors.distillation: Investigate the root cause of the coder_internal agent's repeated failures despite self-healing; possible areas for improvement include better error detection, more robust self-healing mechanisms, or improved initial code generation to minimize errors.

resolved_errors.title: Terminal Bot Inactivity
resolved_errors.summary: The terminal bot skipped actions due to a lack of valid commands in a project designed to create and fix a linting error in a React application.
resolved_errors.distillation: Ensure the planning system provides explicit and actionable terminal commands, especially in tasks requiring specific file modifications or execution steps. Improve error handling to provide feedback when no valid commands are found.

resolved_errors.title: Terminal Bot Inaction on Completion
resolved_errors.summary: The terminal bot skipped its action after the project sequence was completed, indicating a potential failure in handling the final state.
resolved_errors.distillation: Implement a check within the terminal bot to ensure it executes a final verification step or provides a completion message upon project completion, even if no explicit commands are needed. This would prevent skipped actions and offer better feedback.

resolved_errors.title: Agent stalled due to lack of valid commands.
resolved_errors.summary: The agent stalled because it could not identify any valid commands to execute, leading to a skipped action.
resolved_errors.distillation: Ensure agents are equipped with a robust command recognition and execution mechanism, including error handling for situations where no valid commands are applicable to prevent stalling; implement a fallback behavior or alternative action strategy when faced with no valid commands.

resolved_errors.title: Terminal Bot Inaction
resolved_errors.summary: The terminal bot skipped execution due to a lack of valid commands, indicating a potential issue in command generation or task clarity.
resolved_errors.distillation: Ensure that the task decomposition and command generation processes provide clear and executable commands for the terminal bot to prevent inaction.

resolved_errors.title: Agent failed to execute any valid commands.
resolved_errors.summary: The agent failed to execute any valid commands, resulting in a skipped action during the execution of the project sequence.
resolved_errors.distillation: Investigate the reason why no valid commands were generated and ensure the agent can properly parse the instructions and execute relevant commands to complete the task.

resolved_errors.title: Agent Skipping Actions Due to No Valid Commands
resolved_errors.summary: The agent skipped actions because it did not identify any valid commands to execute, resulting in a failed objective.
resolved_errors.distillation: When an agent fails to identify valid commands and skips actions, investigate the command generation logic and ensure that the agent is correctly parsing the environment or task requirements to produce executable commands; review the agent's observation and thought process to identify potential misinterpretations or lack of relevant information.

resolved_errors.title: Simulate and Resolve Linting Errors in React
resolved_errors.summary: The agent successfully created a React component with a linting error related to an undefined variable and then resolved it.
resolved_errors.distillation: To effectively handle linting errors in React projects, intentionally introduce and then resolve errors like undefined variables to reinforce debugging skills and familiarity with linting tools and configurations.

resolved_errors.title: Simulating and Resolving Linting Errors in React
resolved_errors.summary: Demonstrates creating a React component with an undefined variable to trigger a linting error, followed by the process of identifying and resolving the error.
resolved_errors.distillation: To intentionally create a linting error in a React component, introduce an undefined variable; resolve it by either defining the variable or removing its usage. This validates linting tools.

resolved_errors.title: Simulating and Resolving Linting Errors in React
resolved_errors.summary: This trajectory demonstrates how to intentionally introduce and then resolve a linting error in a React application by using an undefined variable.
resolved_errors.distillation: When creating React applications, intentionally introduce linting errors like using undefined variables to ensure linting tools are properly configured and functioning, then resolve these errors to reinforce best practices in code quality and maintainability.

KnowledgeItems.title: Landing Page Structure and Component Breakdown
KnowledgeItems.summary: Provides a structured approach for building a flower shop landing page using React components and Tailwind CSS.
KnowledgeItems.distillation: When creating landing pages, especially for e-commerce or service-oriented businesses, break down the UI into distinct components such as Header, Hero, FeaturedProducts, About, and Footer to ensure modularity and maintainability, and utilize a consistent styling framework like Tailwind CSS for a modern and responsive design.

KnowledgeItems.title: Browser Preview Failure
KnowledgeItems.summary: The browser preview failed, indicating a potential issue with the rendering or deployment of the web application.
KnowledgeItems.distillation: After code generation or modification, always verify browser previews to ensure the application renders correctly and addresses any potential deployment or rendering issues early in the development cycle.

Knowledge Items.title: Implementasi Komponen Inti & Logika Dashboard dengan Tailwind CSS, Recharts, dan Glassmorphism
Knowledge Items.summary: Pola implementasi dashboard keuangan premium melibatkan penggunaan Tailwind CSS untuk styling, Recharts untuk visualisasi data, komponen GlassCard untuk desain glassmorphism, dan arsitektur folder yang bersih dengan TypeScript.
Knowledge Items.distillation: Saat membangun dashboard keuangan premium, inisialisasi Tailwind CSS, buat komponen GlassCard yang dapat digunakan kembali, bangun DashboardLayout dengan sidebar dan area konten, implementasikan Bento Grid di Dashboard, definisikan tipe data keuangan, dan buat komponen chart dengan Recharts.

Knowledge Items.title: Penyebab Timeout pada Trae Builder
Knowledge Items.summary: Timeout pada Trae Builder terjadi ketika tidak ada kode yang terdeteksi dalam jangka waktu tertentu, menandakan potensi masalah dalam penyelesaian tugas oleh coder.
Knowledge Items.distillation: Pastikan coder memiliki waktu yang cukup untuk menyelesaikan tugas dan mengirimkan kode. Jika timeout sering terjadi, pertimbangkan untuk meningkatkan batas waktu atau menganalisis mengapa coder tidak dapat menyelesaikan tugas tepat waktu. Periksa juga apakah ada kesalahan atau masalah lain yang menghalangi coder dari menyelesaikan pekerjaan.

Knowledge_Items.title: Implementasi Komponen UI Dashboard dengan TypeScript dan Glassmorphism
Knowledge_Items.summary: Mengimplementasikan komponen UI utama dashboard (Card, Sidebar, Header, ChartWidget) menggunakan TypeScript dengan desain glassmorphism dan menyusunnya dalam layout Bento Grid.
Knowledge_Items.distillation: Saat membangun dashboard dengan TypeScript dan glassmorphism, susun komponen UI utama seperti Card, Sidebar, Header, dan ChartWidget dalam struktur folder yang terorganisir (src/components), manfaatkan tipe data yang didefinisikan (src/types) untuk props, dan gunakan layout seperti Bento Grid untuk menyusun komponen-komponen tersebut dalam tampilan yang menarik dan responsif.

best_practices.title: Creating a Premium AI Marketing Agency Landing Page
best_practices.summary: Successfully built a premium AI marketing agency landing page with bento grid design, glassmorphism effects, Framer Motion animations, and Lucide icons, using a professional folder structure (hooks, components, services) and strict TypeScript.
best_practices.distillation: When building landing pages with specific design requirements, prioritize upfront planning to select appropriate UI libraries (e.g., Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Lucide Icons) and establish a clear folder structure (hooks, components, services) for maintainability. Utilize TypeScript's strict mode to enhance code quality and prevent potential errors.

best_practices.title: React Typescript Project Setup and Component Generation
best_practices.summary: This trajectory demonstrates a successful setup of a React Typescript project with Vite, including the generation of several components to fulfill the design requirements of a landing page.
best_practices.distillation: When building a React TypeScript project with a specific design in mind (e.g., bento grid, glassmorphism), initiate the project using Vite and immediately structure the folder with 'hooks', 'components', and 'services' directories; then, create the core components (Header, Hero, BentoCard, BentoGrid, Footer) and define the necessary types for type safety using index.ts in the types directory.

best_practices.title: React Native Tamagui Project Initialization
best_practices.summary: The agent successfully initialized a React Native project using `create-vite` with the react-ts template and installed dependencies.
best_practices.distillation: When starting a new React Native project with Tamagui, use `npx -y create-vite@latest ./ --template react-ts --no-interactive && npm install` for quick setup and dependency installation.

Knowledge_Items.title: Successful Website Creation Trajectory
Knowledge_Items.summary: The agent successfully created a full website store through a sequence of terminal commands and internal code generation, followed by a browser preview.
Knowledge_Items.distillation: The pattern involves utilizing terminal commands for environment setup and file manipulation, internal code generation for implementing website functionality, and finally, browser preview for visual verification. Prioritize efficient terminal command execution and code generation to optimize website creation.

best_practices.title: Vite React TypeScript Template Initialization
best_practices.summary: Using 'create-vite' with the React TypeScript template provides a quick and efficient way to scaffold a new React project.
best_practices.distillation: When starting a new React TypeScript project, use 'npx -y create-vite@latest ./ --template react-ts --no-interactive && npm install' to quickly set up the project with Vite and install the necessary dependencies, streamlining the initial setup process.

KnowledgeItem.title: Agent Inaction Due to No Valid Commands
KnowledgeItem.summary: The agent skipped execution because no valid commands were available given the current state.
KnowledgeItem.distillation: When the agent reports 'No valid commands', review the planning and ensure that the generated plan contains actions that are executable within the agent's available tools and current environment.

best_practices.title: React Typescript Vite Project Initialization
best_practices.summary: Utilize create-vite with the react-ts template and npm install for efficient React Typescript project setup.
best_practices.distillation: When initiating a new React Typescript project, streamline the process by using `npx -y create-vite@latest ./ --template react-ts --no-interactive` followed by `npm install` to quickly scaffold the project and install dependencies.
